title: Financial Frictions, Innovation Signals, and SME Credit Allocation
summary: This paper examines how public innovation-related signals reduce screening costs for lenders and improve the allocation of bank credit to innovative SMEs.

title: Technology Markets and Firm Financing Constraints: New Evidence
summary: The study links technology factor markets to improved monetization of intangible assets and lower precautionary cash holding among patent-intensive firms.

title: Trade Credit, Information Transparency, and Innovation Investment
summary: Using panel evidence, the authors show that better information transparency strengthens the complementarity between formal finance and trade credit in supporting innovation.

title: Public Information Infrastructure and Bank Screening of Innovative Firms
summary: This article studies how public information platforms can improve banks’ screening efficiency and ease financing constraints faced by small innovative firms.

title: Disclosure Quality, Supplier Financing, and Innovation Persistence
summary: The paper explores whether higher-quality corporate disclosure enhances supplier financing relationships and sustains long-run innovation activity.

title: Patent Pledge Financing and R&D Dynamics in High-Tech SMEs
summary: This paper examines whether patent pledge financing reshapes the timing and intensity of R&D investment among high-tech small and medium-sized enterprises.

title: Innovation Finance and the Allocation of Credit under Information Asymmetry
journal: Economics of Innovation and New Technology
summary: The article develops evidence on how targeted innovation finance mechanisms can improve credit allocation when banks face severe information asymmetry.

title: Bank Competition, Soft Information, and Innovation Lending
summary: This study investigates the role of market structure and soft information in shaping bank lending to innovative firms.

title: Technology Signals, Credit Rationing, and Entrepreneurial Growth
summary: The paper shows how observable technology signals can reduce credit rationing and foster entrepreneurial growth in young firms.

title: Formal Finance, Trade Credit, and Innovation Complementarity
summary: This article analyzes whether formal finance and trade credit become more complementary when firms invest in uncertain innovation projects.

title: Policy Coordination and Financial Support for Innovative SMEs
summary: The study discusses how policy coordination across government departments improves financial support systems for innovative SMEs.

title: Information Disclosure Timeliness and Innovation Outcomes
summary: This paper links more timely information disclosure to improved innovation outcomes through lower financing frictions and better market confidence.
